que: In Kotlin Type Casting, what is the primary goal?
options: skip state/data modeling
replace testing entirely

options: random utility class name
is / as?
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic

optimize before verifying correctness

options: forcing unsafe casts everywhere

que: For production code, which approach fits Kotlin Type Casting best?
options: Use Kotlin Type Casting only as theory with no implementation

que: A teammate asks where to start with Kotlin Type Casting. What is best advice?
options: Read official docs (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/typecasts.html) and implement a small sample
avoid feedback from tests/review
copy an unknown snippet without context
skip running the app after changes

que: Which statement about Kotlin Type Casting is most accurate?
options: Kotlin Type Casting should be applied identically in every layer
Kotlin Type Casting is mainly about adding boilerplate
Kotlin Type Casting removes the need for any testing
Kotlin Type Casting is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data

que: In a code review for Kotlin Type Casting, what would you flag?
options: small pure functions with clear names
traceable data flow between layers
explicit modeling of UI/data states
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Kotlin Type Casting correctly?
options: a large refactor without tests
a slide deck with no runnable code
an implementation with no error states
A small, working feature that demonstrates is / as? in context

que: To learn Kotlin Type Casting deeply, what should you do next?
options: skip verification to save time
memorize definitions only
avoid official references
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Kotlin Type Casting patterns
